Revolutionizing Tanzanian Agriculture

22 Aug, 2023  ·  Admin

In a groundbreaking development for Tanzanian agriculture, the MazaoHub team of extension officers has successfully conducted soil testing on 190 soil samples in a single weekend using state-of-the-art MazaoHub Soil Scanners — within just three days. This achievement has paved the way for innovative recommendations to be made for each of these samples. Moreover, each soil sample analysis comes at an affordable price of TSh 30,000 or less, depending on the quantity of samples tested. This initiative is set to transform farming practices across Tanzania and ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.

MazaoHub's Soil Scanning advancements

MazaoHub's Soil Scanners represent a significant leap in soil analysis technology. These scanners offer not only precision but also speed, enabling our extension officers to provide prompt recommendations to farmers. These recommendations are essential for optimizing crop production, as they take into account the specific characteristics of the soil, the type of crop, the climate, and the unique needs of each farmer.

Tomorrow, our dedicated team is heading to Ruvuma to meet with a staggering 10,000 farmers. In a gesture of support, we will provide Soil Scanners to the extension officers in Ruvuma, empowering them to serve the local farming community effectively. Each farmer tested with the MazaoHub Soil Scanner will benefit from a personalized and automated agronomy service, powered by artificial intelligence.

The process: from soil testing to harvesting

Farmers who undergo soil testing with MazaoHub are embarking on a transformative journey. They receive guidance at every step — from soil analysis to land preparation, planting, nurturing, and harvesting. This guidance ensures that each farmer maximizes their crop yield while practicing sustainable and efficient farming methods.

Furthermore, MazaoHub's mobile application allows farmers to record all their farming activities, thereby maintaining comprehensive records of their agricultural endeavors. These records are invaluable for securing loans and financing, as responsible and well-documented farming practices make farmers more attractive to financial institutions.

Farmers Secretly Abandoning Tradition for a Revolutionary Agricultural App

25 Aug, 2023  ·  Admin

Introduction

In the heart of Tanzania's agricultural landscape, a quiet revolution is taking place that challenges age-old farming practices and promises to reshape the future of agriculture. MazaoHub, an innovative agricultural platform, has ignited discussions and controversies, particularly surrounding its profound impact on climate-sensitive practices. As the world grapples with the challenges of a changing climate, MazaoHub stands as a bold contender offering transformative solutions to farmers.

AI Agronomy for precise farming

MazaoHub's AI Agronomy represents a pioneering leap in providing comprehensive guidance to farmers at every step of their agricultural journey. Through a seamless integration of cutting-edge technology and agricultural expertise, MazaoHub ensures that farmers are empowered with the knowledge and tools they need to achieve optimal results. From the moment a farmer registers their field on the platform, MazaoHub becomes a dedicated partner, guiding them through each phase of cultivation with unrivaled precision.

Imagine a farmer waking up to a notification on their mobile device, reminding them of the tasks that need attention in their registered field. MazaoHub's AI-driven system leverages data from soil tests, weather conditions, and crop specifics to generate tailored recommendations. These recommendations encompass a wide spectrum of activities — from land preparation techniques to sowing methods, nutrient application, pest and disease management, and irrigation strategies. Farmers are guided on when and how to carry out these tasks, effectively following best practices to optimize their crop yield.

Moreover, MazaoHub's commitment to farmers doesn't end at the harvest. The platform provides guidance on proper post-harvest procedures, storage methods, and even helps farmers navigate the intricacies of marketing their produce. The traceability report generated by MazaoHub offers an invaluable tool for off-takers and buyers seeking to access products from the platform — providing a comprehensive overview of the cultivation process, ensuring transparency and quality assurance.

MazaoHub's prowess lies in its role as a complete extension management system. It transcends the limitations of traditional agricultural extension services by offering a holistic approach that covers the entire farming journey — from the first soil test to the final sale.

Disrupting Agricultural Norms and Challenging Climate Fate in Africa

17 Sep, 2023  ·  Admin

Introduction

In the heart of Tanzania's agricultural landscape, a quiet revolution is taking place that challenges age-old farming practices and promises to reshape the future of agriculture. MazaoHub, an innovative agricultural platform, has ignited discussions and controversies, particularly surrounding its profound impact on climate-sensitive practices. As the world grapples with the challenges of a changing climate, MazaoHub stands as a bold contender offering transformative solutions to farmers.

MazaoHub AI Agronomy module

Think about this challenge: on average in Tanzania and other African countries, one extension officer is responsible for managing over 3,000 smallholder farmers in their village — and they do this manually. They are legally required to visit 7 farmers per day and record information with pen and paper. They must provide agronomic advice and track the progress of each farmer, which can be difficult manually, and sometimes they forget what advice they gave. As a result, many farmers are left without help, leading to trial-and-error farming, poor-quality produce, and low productivity.

MazaoHub AI Agronomy is the first module of MazaoHub Climate Farm Management Software, serving as its foundation. It uses AI technology to streamline personalized agronomy services for each farmer, bridging the gap and easing communication between farmers, remote extension officers and decision makers — including farm planning, cost analysis, and projections. It streamlines activities from field mapping, land preparation, sowing, fertilizer application, pest control, and more, all tailored to the farmer based on the specific village, soil, crop, climate, and other factors.

Imagine a farmer or extension officer receiving notifications on their mobile device, reminding them of tasks in their fields. MazaoHub's AI system uses data from soil tests, weather conditions, and crop details to provide customized guidance on various farming activities, from planting to harvesting. This eliminates the need for pen and paper, and extension officers can monitor farmers' progress using their phones, powered by AI technology.

MazaoHub doesn't stop at harvest; it also offers guidance on post-harvest procedures, storage, and marketing. The platform's traceability report is valuable for buyers, ensuring transparency and quality assurance throughout the cultivation process. MazaoHub is more than just AI Agronomy; it's a complete extension management system.

15 Global Farm Management Software Influencing Carbon Farming Practices

18 Sep, 2023  ·  Admin

Introduction

In our quest for a greener and more sustainable planet, the concept of carbon farming has emerged as a promising solution. It revolves around a simple yet powerful idea: using our farms and fields to capture and store carbon from the atmosphere. This innovative approach has the potential to address several pressing environmental challenges while also boosting our economies.

The Earth's soil holds a vast treasure trove of carbon — approximately 680 gigatons — which plays a crucial role in maintaining the delicate balance of our climate. Carbon farming practices have the potential to significantly increase these soil carbon stocks by up to 10%. This seemingly modest improvement could translate into an astounding 68 gigatons of carbon sequestration on a global scale, equivalent to mitigating 17 years' worth of global greenhouse gas emissions.

What's even more intriguing is the socio-economic impact of carbon farming. According to a study conducted by the World Resources Institute, this innovative agricultural practice could create up to 20 million new jobs worldwide by the year 2030 — meaning not only a greener Earth but also improved livelihoods for millions of people around the world.

What is carbon farming?

Carbon farming, also known as biosequestration, is a set of agricultural practices that aim to remove carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and store it in the soil. This can be done by planting trees, cover crops, and other vegetation, as well as by using management practices that improve soil health. It is a natural solution to climate change because it helps reduce the amount of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere: when carbon dioxide is stored in the soil, it cannot trap heat and warm the planet.

In addition to its climate benefits, carbon farming also offers a number of other advantages:

  • Improved soil health: carbon farming practices help improve soil structure, increase water-holding capacity, and boost fertility.
  • Higher, more resilient yields: healthier soils produce more, and withstand drought and extreme weather better.
  • New income streams: measurable carbon removal can be converted into carbon credits for sale.

This is the frontier MazaoHub is building toward in Africa — combining soil testing, regenerative agronomy and AI to help smallholder farmers practice carbon farming and one day earn from the carbon they store.

MazaoHub Services Overview

24 Nov, 2023  ·  Admin

MazaoHub delivers Climate Farm Management Software as a Service, combining AI-powered agronomy, soil science and on-the-ground support. Here is an overview of our services.

AI-Powered Agronomy Services

Geospatial Data: The MazaoHub App allows users to map their farms — including crops, farm boundaries, climatological details, water availability, soil type and temperature. This enables farmers to estimate and analyze potential crop productivity and risk exposure, crucial for informed decision-making.

Weather Forecast: MazaoHub collaborates with meteorological agencies to provide farmers with accurate weather forecasts tailored to their specific locations and villages, aiding better decisions on seeds and farming practices.

GAP (Good Agricultural Practices): MazaoHub Agronomy provides tailored production guidelines for each crop in every village. AI guides farmers with bespoke farming practices through every stage of production — from land preparation, sowing plan, fertilization and pesticide programs to harvesting, post-harvest practices and storage.

Crop Monitoring: MazaoHub uses satellite imagery and remote sensing to analyze changes in plant growth, plant health, vegetation index (NDVI) and nutrient needs, providing farmers with simplified interpretations and recommendations at each stage to mitigate risks effectively.

Carbon Farming and Regenerative Guide: An AI-powered extension service that helps farmers implement regenerative agriculture and carbon farming practices, working with extension officers from Farmer Support Centers to provide on-the-ground assistance and calculate carbon removal credits for each farm.

Soil Analysis Services

Soil Analysis and Recommendations: We produce our own Soil Sensors and distribute them to our Farmer Support Centers (FSC) and agri-dealers. Farmers can visit or submit soil samples to a nearby FSC for analysis, receiving recommendations within 5 minutes for crop selection, fertilizer usage (how much and how to use it) and soil amendments needed. This aids climate adaptation and enhances productivity and sustainability.

Facilitate Inputs Distribution

Through our franchise model — Farmer Support Centers (FSCs) — MazaoHub ensures the distribution of quality inputs to farmers within our network, partnering with large input suppliers to bring the right products closer to the farm.

Building a Better Tomorrow: How MazaoHub Is Empowering African Farmers

04 Dec, 2023  ·  Admin

Imagine a continent where smallholder farmers — the backbone of Africa's agricultural sector — are no longer plagued by limited information, fragmented value chains, and the whims of a changing climate. Imagine them empowered with personalized knowledge, connected to markets, and equipped to thrive. This isn't a utopia — it's the future MazaoHub is building, one byte at a time.

A climate-smart revolution

MazaoHub is more than just farm management software; it's a revolution. It's a climate-smart platform that integrates soil sensors with AI-powered agronomy, giving farmers bespoke insights from fertilizer recommendations to carbon farming guides. This isn't science fiction — it's changing lives.

A market ripe for disruption

Africa is home to over 330 million smallholder farmers, cultivating 80% of the farmland and contributing 70% of food production. In Tanzania alone, 13.5 million smallholder farmers are the backbone of the agricultural sector. Yet they face immense challenges:

  • Limited access to information: traditional extension services are stretched thin, leaving farmers with outdated knowledge.
  • Fragmented value chains: farmers struggle to connect with buyers, often stuck with middlemen who take a significant cut.
  • Climate change: erratic weather and unpredictable rainfall threaten yields and livelihoods.

MazaoHub: a beacon of hope

Its unique features address every pain point, empowering farmers like never before:

  • Offline and online accessibility: whether a farmer has a smartphone or a basic feature phone, MazaoHub is there.
  • Real-time soil data: soil sensors provide instant insights into soil health, enabling data-driven decisions about fertilization and amendments.
  • AI-powered agronomy: MazaoHub personalizes recommendations for every farmer, considering their unique soil, climate, and crop choices.
  • Carbon farming guidance: farmers can tap into the carbon market with automated guides tailored to their location and crops.
  • Comprehensive record-keeping and analytics: track expenses, analyze performance, and make decisions based on hard data.
  • Integrated POS and financial reports: managing finances becomes effortless with a built-in POS and automated reports, empowering farmers to secure loans and grow.

MazaoHub: Revolutionizing Agriculture with AI and Empowering FPOs & FPCs

21 Dec, 2023  ·  Admin

Imagine a world where farmers wield the power of technology, where soil whispers valuable secrets, and every crop flourishes under a watchful eye. This is the future MazaoHub is forging — a future where agriculture dances with cutting-edge AI, propelling smallholder farmers and their cooperatives, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) and Farmer Producer Companies (FPCs), to new heights of success.

MazaoHub isn't just a farm management platform; it's a revolution disguised as software. It's a tireless agronomist, a vigilant guardian, a finance and logistics facilitator, and a savvy business advisor, all rolled into one.

Sensors, satellites, and drones

MazaoHub doesn't work in the dark. It gathers intelligence from a constellation of digital tools:

  • Soil sensors: these intrepid little devices burrow deep, whispering back precious secrets about nutrients, carbon content, moisture, and pH.
  • Satellites: like eagle-eyed sentinels in the sky, they keep watch over weather patterns, vegetation health, and even pests and diseases, with high-resolution imagery of every inch of your land.
  • Drones: nimble aerial scouts provide in-depth crop health assessments, spotting even the subtlest signs of trouble before they blossom into full-blown woes.
  • Mobile app: farmers become active participants, recording GPS mapping, planting dates, costs, and other farm activities.

But all this data is just raw material. MazaoHub's AI, the true maestro, orchestrates a symphony of algorithms, transforming this information into bespoke recommendations tailored to every farmer's unique needs.

Personalized care for every farm

Imagine this: you wake up to a detailed report on your phone — not from a horoscope app, but from MazaoHub. It tells you precisely which fertilizer to apply to which plot, when to water your crops, and exactly how to act at each stage — turning every smallholder farm, cooperative and FPO into a data-driven, thriving business.

A Look at Africa's Sugar Industry — and How MazaoHub Can Sweeten the Deal

21 Dec, 2023  ·  Admin

Sugar, oh sugar! It's more than just a sweetener in our tea; it's a vital part of Africa's agricultural landscape. While not every country is a sugar powerhouse, some are rising to the top. Let's take a peek at the top 3 sugar producers in Africa:

  1. South Africa: the undisputed champion, producing a whopping 2.2 million tons of sugar each year.
  2. Egypt: not far behind, with a respectable 1.8 million tons annually.
  3. Eswatini (formerly Swaziland): don't let their small size fool you — they produce around 500,000 tons of sugar yearly.

But what about Tanzania? Tanzania is on the sweet path, with around 350,000 tons annually, steadily climbing the sugar ladder. But even with this progress, challenges remain. Farming sugar isn't a cakewalk — drought, pests, and unpredictable weather can leave even the sweetest sugarcane feeling sour.

That's where MazaoHub's Farm Management Software comes in, acting like a friendly farmer's assistant — a magic toolbox bursting with smart tools to help every step of the way, from planting the first seed to delivering the sweetest sugar.

Agricultural Management System

  • Crop monitoring and yield estimation: MazaoHub leverages satellite imagery, drones, and sensors to monitor crop health, predict yields, and optimize resource allocation.
  • Pest and disease detection and control: early detection and targeted interventions become possible with AI-powered disease prediction and weather forecasting.
  • Soil management and analysis: optimize fertilizer and water usage with precise soil mapping and nutrient analysis, reducing costs and boosting soil health.
  • Farm record keeping and traceability: full digital records from field to factory, giving millers and exporters the traceable, reliable supply they need.

Unearthing the Hidden Gems of Agriculture in Tanzania

01 Jan, 2024  ·  Admin

Imagine a land where lush fields dance under the sun, kissed by the gentle breeze of the Serengeti. This is Tanzania, a nation where the soil whispers stories of ancient baobabs and dreams of bountiful harvests. But beneath the familiar narrative of savannah and wildlife lies a hidden treasure: the vibrant tapestry of agriculture.

For generations, Tanzanian hands have nurtured the earth, coaxing life from rich, red soil. Agriculture isn't just a livelihood; it's the heartbeat of the nation, a rhythmic pulse that sustains over 70% of the population. Yet, despite its vital role, agriculture often gets overshadowed — misunderstood as a dusty field reserved for the uneducated and underprivileged. Let's shed that dusty image. Tanzanian agriculture is not a relic of the past; it's a dynamic landscape buzzing with innovation and opportunity.

A land of abundance: Tanzania boasts fertile land, diverse climates, and a generous sun. From the coffee-kissed slopes of Kilimanjaro to the cassava fields of Mbeya, a wide range of crops thrive — tea, cotton, cashew nuts, wheat, fruits, vegetables and spices — a vibrant symphony of flavors that paint Tanzania on the global culinary canvas.

Hidden wealth in every seed: agriculture is not just about subsistence. It's a goldmine waiting to be unearthed. Imagine transforming a humble grain of maize into a protein bar, or picture ripe bananas reborn as a delicious, exportable smoothie. The possibilities are endless, waiting to be unlocked by creative minds and entrepreneurial spirit.

Technology takes root: gone are the days of back-breaking labor and unpredictable yields. Technology is transforming the agricultural landscape, from drones that monitor crops to smart irrigation systems that optimize water use. Data-driven farming, once a futuristic dream, is becoming a reality — empowering farmers to make informed decisions and maximize profits.

Young farmers, big dreams: the perception of agriculture as a field for the uneducated is rapidly fading. A new generation of tech-savvy young farmers is taking root — and with platforms like MazaoHub in their hands, they are unearthing the hidden gems of Tanzanian agriculture for the world.

A Comprehensive Model for Farm Support Centres

08 Feb, 2024  ·  Admin

MazaoHub Precision Agriculture Inc. introduces Kliniki za Kilimo (Swahili for "Farm Support Centers"). Currently we have 30 Farmer Support Centers led by 30 extension officers, reaching over 34,000 farmers. These centers, powered by MazaoHub's comprehensive suite of Farm Management Software, empower rural farmers with knowledge, resources, and technology — leading to increased productivity, income, and sustainability. This model outlines the operation of Kliniki za Kilimo, highlighting its unique features, partnerships, and impact potential.

A holistic approach to farmer support

Kliniki za Kilimo are more than just physical centers; they are hubs of knowledge, technology, markets and collaboration, operating under the following core principles:

  • Precision Agriculture: MazaoHub's Farm ERP, soil sensors, IoT devices, drones, and satellite data capture real-time farm data, analyzed by AI-powered software to provide personalized agronomy and GAP recommendations tailored to each farm's context.
  • Farmer Support: Kliniki za Kilimo are led by MazaoHub Extension Officers — trained agricultural professionals who provide on-site and remote guidance on crop selection, soil health, pest and disease control, and market access.
  • Technology Integration: MazaoHub's Farm Record Keeping app facilitates data collection and analysis, enabling traceability, financial reports, geospatial data capture for climate action, and farm management optimization.

Key services offered

  • Soil Testing and Analysis: actionable insights into soil health and nutrient deficiencies, allowing informed fertilization decisions, using MazaoHub technologies.
  • Crop Management Guidance: AI-powered, customized crop recommendations including planting schedules, irrigation plans, and pest and disease control strategies.
  • Market Linkages: connecting farmers directly to buyers and processors via MazaoHub's e-commerce platform, ensuring fair prices.
  • Financial Access: partnerships with banks and financial institutions facilitate access to loans and other financial services.
  • Training and Capacity Building: Kliniki za Kilimo organize regular workshops and training to keep farmers ahead.

Field Notes  /  Funding

Seeds of Appreciation, Harvest of Success

08 Feb, 2024  ·  Admin

2023 — what a ride! It's been a year of whirlwind learning, of opportunities blossoming like sunflowers under the Tanzanian sun, and yes, of challenges that tested our mettle like sturdy baobab trees in a storm. But through it all, one thing stands tall: gratitude.

We want to start by saying a massive "Asante!" to the universe, to God, for guiding our steps and whispering courage in our ears when the path seemed uncertain. It's your hand we feel on our shoulders, pushing us forward when doubt threatened to creep in.

Next, a big "Thank you!" to the Government of Tanzania. You saw the spark in our eyes, the potential in our digital seeds, and chose to nurture them. You believed in a bunch of young dreamers, and that is a gift beyond measure.

Our connector, Kirenga

Our first encounter came when we were introduced to the Ministry of Agriculture — thank you, Mr. Godfrey Kirenga. We cannot forget the day Minister Bashe introduced us to President Samia Suluhu Hassan. The earth seemed to shift beneath our feet, excitement blooming like a thousand sunflowers. Suddenly, our small dream felt colossal, our responsibility immense. But Minister Bashe's trust, like a gentle breeze, nurtured that excitement into conviction.

Meeting the President

A special thank you to President Samia Suluhu Hassan, whose unwavering commitment to agriculture has allowed MazaoHub to reach over 33,000 farmers. The investments in irrigation schemes and the emphasis on bringing farmers together have allowed us to easily digitize farmers using MazaoHub — these are the fertile grounds where our seeds have blossomed. And as we look towards the future, one wish burns bright: continued investment in agricultural infrastructure.

Field Notes  /  Product

10 Global Farm Management Software for Smallholder Farmers: A Bicycle or a Ferrari?

24 Feb, 2024  ·  Admin

James, a passionate smallholder farmer, sighed deeply and leaned back in his chair. We were having chai, discussing the rise of farm management software. His frustration filled the air — he was objecting to using MazaoHub Farm Software.

"It's like giving a farmer a Ferrari when what they truly need is a reliable bicycle," he exclaimed. "There are so many farm software or farm ERPs out there, but none seem to truly understand the challenges faced by small-scale farmers in Africa. They're complicated, expensive, and often designed for giant farms in other countries. It's like trying to wear shoes that are ten sizes too big!"

So, what sets MazaoHub Farm Software apart — or is it just another 'me too' farm software popular in the United States and Europe but unsuitable for our farming environment?

I nodded, understanding his pain. James, like many other small-scale farmers here in Africa, felt left behind by the technological wave. These flashy, complex software solutions, designed for vast commercial farms in distant lands, felt like oversized shoes — impossible to wear, let alone run in.

Let us embark on a journey together, exploring different farming management software, their origins, and how they could potentially address the specific challenges faced by farmers like James. But before we begin our trek, let's delve deeper into James' initial statement, understanding the heart of this quest for the perfect tool.

Remember the image of the bicycle — simple, dependable, and able to navigate any terrain. This is the essence of what James, and countless other small-scale farmers in Africa, seek: not a technological marvel out of reach, but a down-to-earth companion on their agricultural journey.

As we analyze each candidate's features and origin stories, we keep James' vision for a "farmer's bicycle" in mind — and we will see whether any of these software solutions can truly stand out from the crowd, earning the title of the simple, dependable tool that Africa's smallholder farmers actually need. That is exactly the bar MazaoHub was built to meet.

Share

Field Notes  /  Story

Today, I Visited My Grandmother's Farm

01 May, 2024  ·  Admin

Today, I visited my grandmother's farm, a place etched deeply in our family history. This small plot, ten kilometers from our home, sustained us for generations. We spent countless weekends here, toiling the land and harvesting crops — the very crops that paid for my education 30 years ago. Yet, a shadow hangs over this cherished place: the farming methods haven't changed in three decades.

No extension officers visit the village, offering guidance on best practices for fertilizers, pesticides, or overall crop management. This not only impacts their harvest but also potentially threatens their health.

Adding to their struggles, a natural wonder lies dormant beneath their feet. A rock bottom, just 60 feet deep, diverts precious water flowing from Mount Kilimanjaro. Just across the border, in Kenya, our neighbors use this very water source to cultivate thriving vegetable gardens, leading to multiple harvests and a better life. My grandmother's village, however, relies solely on unpredictable and scarce rainfall.

But hope blooms alongside the challenges. I believe MazaoHub can be the catalyst for change. This innovative platform connects small farmers with agricultural experts and cutting-edge technology. MazaoHub can equip them with knowledge on best practices, guide them towards optimal fertilizer and pesticide use, and connect them to water, markets and finance — so that the next generation farming this land will not be left behind as mine nearly was.

Share

Field Notes  /  Soil

We Are Thrilled to Unveil Our Brand-New Soil Sensors

01 May, 2024  ·  Admin

From our humble beginnings to where we stand today, we've traversed a remarkable path filled with milestones and achievements. We are thrilled to unveil our brand-new Soil Sensors — a testament to our unwavering commitment to innovation and progress.

Our initial Soil Sensors have undergone a significant transformation, reflecting our dedication to continuous improvement. We've expanded the range of parameters measured by our sensors, making them even more versatile and valuable tools for farmers.

This remarkable progress has been made possible, in part, by the invaluable support of Catalyst Fund. Their investment has empowered us to refine our Soil Sensors, enhancing their ease of use for extension officers who serve farmers across the region.

Empowered by Catalyst Fund's investment, we are poised to expand our production capacity, ensuring that our innovative Soil Sensors reach even more farmers. By the end of May, we anticipate distributing over 100 of these new designs, further amplifying our impact on the agricultural landscape. We extend our deepest gratitude to Catalyst Fund for their unwavering belief in our mission.

Share

Field Notes  /  Farm Clinics

We Are Expanding Our Farmer Excellence Centers

01 May, 2024  ·  Admin

We are excited, as MazaoHub, to share this update. We are happy to announce the expansion of our Farmer Excellence Centers — popularly known as Kliniki za Kilimo to farmers in Swahili — to better serve farmers in Ifakara and surrounding locations. With the addition of five new centers, we are now able to provide comprehensive agricultural support to farmers in Kilombero, Ifakara, Mlimba, Malinyi, and all surrounding villages and wards, reaching 70,000 farmers.

All of these Farmer Excellence Centers are located in rural farming locations, led by our extension officers.

Our services include:

  1. Soil testing and fertilizer recommendations: our experts analyse soil samples using our sensors to determine nutrient deficiencies and recommend the appropriate fertilizers to optimize crop growth and yield.
  2. Farm mapping: we use advanced technology to create detailed maps of each farm, enabling farmers and financial institutions to make informed decisions about land use, irrigation, and crop management.
  3. AI-powered crop health monitoring: our AI-powered tools continuously monitor crops for signs of stress, diseases, or pests, allowing farmers to take timely action to prevent yield losses.
  4. Personalized herbicide and pesticide recommendations: based on the specific needs of the crops and the identified threats, we recommend the most effective and environmentally friendly products to protect farmers' yields.

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

A Transformative Pilot to Facilitate Finance to Rural Farmers

01 May, 2024  ·  Admin

MazaoHub AgClimate Limited, a climate data-driven agritech company, is partnering with financial institutions. This collaboration aims to empower more than 15,000 pilot farmers across 7 Farmer Excellence Centers (popularly known as Kliniki za Kilimo in Swahili) during the next farming season.

Farmer Excellence Centers serve as central hubs equipped with:

  • Agricultural ICT tools (smartphones, computers, tablets)
  • Low-cost, solar-powered soil sensors
  • The simple-to-use MazaoHub crop health detection app
  • Post-harvest tools
  • Transportation (motorcycles)
  • Field officers and agronomists

These centers are like health diagnostic clinics — for diagnosis and prescription in farming — that provide:

  • AI-powered agronomic guidance
  • Soil analysis services
  • Crop monitoring
  • Weather forecasts
  • Pesticide application plans
  • Farm record-keeping and data collection
  • Resilient seed distribution
  • Loan monitoring throughout the production cycle

Benefits for farmers

  • Access to finance: agriculture-specific loans tailored to various stages of the value chain, with flexible terms and affordable interest rates.
  • Reduced risk and increased profitability: leveraging MazaoHub's technology and expertise to ensure loan adherence, timely repayments, and reduced defaults.
  • Weather and crop insurance: protection against financial losses due to unpredictable weather events, pests, and diseases.
  • Loan monitoring and repayment: tracked digitally throughout the season, giving lenders confidence and farmers a fair, transparent path to credit.

Field Notes  /  Soil

Soil Is the Mother

08 May, 2024  ·  Admin

Soil: the cradle of crops

Soil is a complex ecosystem teeming with life — a delicate balance of minerals, organic matter, and microorganisms. It acts as a natural storehouse of nutrients, providing essential elements like nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium, which are crucial for plant growth and development. These nutrients, taken up by roots, fuel the photosynthetic process, enabling plants to produce the sugars and starches that form the basis of our food chain.

Understanding crop nutrient requirements

Different crops have varying nutrient demands, dictated by their unique genetic makeup and growth patterns. For instance, cereal crops like maize and rice require high levels of nitrogen to support their vigorous foliage and grain production. On the other hand, legumes such as beans and peas have the remarkable ability to fix nitrogen from the air, reducing the need for external fertilization.

Soil amendments: restoring and enhancing fertility

Over time, soil's nutrient reserves can deplete, leading to a decline in crop yields. To address this, soil amendments are introduced to replenish essential elements and restore fertility. Common amendments include organic matter like compost and manure, which not only add nutrients but also improve soil structure and water retention. In other cases, inorganic fertilizers such as urea and superphosphate are used to provide a quick boost of specific nutrients.

MazaoHub: empowering farmers with technology

MazaoHub, an innovative farming software and Tech & Touch model, revolutionizes the way farmers manage their crops and optimize productivity. By leveraging geospatial data, soil testing, and expert agronomy, MazaoHub gives every farmer a precise prescription for their soil — so the mother of all crops is fed exactly what she needs, and nothing she doesn't.

Share

Field Notes  /  Farm Clinics

Transforming Rural Farmers with Farmer Excellence Centers

20 May, 2024  ·  Admin

A personal journey

I am a smallholder farmer, born and raised in a family of small-scale farmers. I have experienced the trials and tribulations of farming firsthand. I've seen poverty up close and felt the isolation that comes from a lack of extension services. My farming journey has been a continuous cycle of trial and error. I have destroyed crops, lost significant investments, and faced hunger and hardship. Even the little I managed to produce was often exploited by middlemen.

If you asked me today, tomorrow, or the day after, I would tell you that inefficient and disorganized agricultural extension services — which are not data-driven and often assumed to be free — have failed farmers for generations. This persistent problem has kept African farmers impoverished year after year. Despite numerous projects, donations, and grants aimed at helping smallholder farmers, their situation remains unchanged.

Seeing an extension officer from MazaoHub's Farmer Excellence Center — popularly known among farmers as Kliniki ya Kilimo — visiting a farmer like Juma with the necessary inputs, soil sensors and the software, gives me hope and peace. It reassures me that I am doing something meaningful for my fellow farmers.

The birth of MazaoHub

MazaoHub was created directly for smallholder farmers and indirectly for large commercial farmers and plantations. However, the big truth is that having a Farm ERP or farm management software to manage day-to-day farm operations, along with AI-powered extension services without on-ground support, will never truly reach and impact the farmer's daily agricultural business. As a founder who has lived through the challenges of small-scale farming, I witnessed the need for a system that provides practical, on-the-ground support. That's why we established the Farmer Excellence Centers.

What are Farmer Excellence Centers?

Farmer Excellence Centers — Kliniki ya Kilimo in Swahili — are trusted hubs dedicated to supporting farmers with tailored services and trusted, on-the-ground support: soil testing, AI agronomy, inputs, market access and finance, all in one place close to the farm.

Share

Field Notes  /  Funding

MazaoHub's New Investment from Nordic Impact Funds

12 Aug, 2024  ·  Admin

A revolution in farming

Imagine walking into a clinic — not for yourself, but for your farm. This is the reality that MazaoHub is bringing to over 50,000 farmers across Tanzania and beyond. On the auspicious occasion of Nane Nane Day, a celebration of agriculture in Tanzania, MazaoHub has announced an exciting new investment from Nordic Impact Funds. This investment is set to revolutionize farming in Tanzania, providing smallholder farmers with the tools and technologies needed to thrive. The heart of this transformation lies in the establishment of Farmer Excellence Centers, known locally as Kliniki za Kilimo, which are poised to become the nerve centers of precision farming in Africa.

Farmer Excellence Centers: clinics for farms and farmers

At the core of MazaoHub's operations are the Farmer Excellence Centers, envisioned as comprehensive "clinics" for farms. These centers are not just places where farmers can purchase inputs; they are diagnostic and prescriptive hubs where every aspect of farming is meticulously monitored and managed. Picture a clinic where soil health is diagnosed using cutting-edge MazaoHub Soil Sensors, where each farm's data is mapped from farm coordinates, polygons, climate history, water availability and crop suitability using a simple app and satellite imagery. From the collected data, tailored farming solutions are prescribed using AI and advanced agronomy software for each individual farm. This is the future of farming, and it is happening now.

The power of precision

MazaoHub's soil sensors are at the forefront of this precision revolution — bringing lab-grade soil diagnosis to the village, in minutes, at a price smallholder farmers can afford, and pairing it with AI-driven prescriptions delivered by trained extension officers.

Share

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

Elea Foundation's Due Diligence Visit to MazaoHub

31 Oct, 2024  ·  Admin

Introduction

Our recent visit to Ifakara marked a pivotal moment as representatives from Elea Foundation joined us for a due diligence process to gain deeper insights into MazaoHub's core activities and evaluate our impact on smallholder farmers in rural areas. This visit served not only as a platform to showcase the transparency and readiness of our operations but also as a testament to our ability to meet the expectations of investors and partners seeking impactful ventures. Elea Foundation had the opportunity to witness firsthand how MazaoHub is transforming agriculture through cutting-edge digital solutions and precision farming technologies.

MazaoHub's vision is clear: to empower farmers with tools, data, and support systems that help them overcome productivity barriers while enhancing their livelihoods. Our approach blends Tech and Touch to deliver data-driven agronomy services through AI-powered agronomy solutions, geospatial data systems, and soil sensors. During this visit, representatives from Elea Foundation gained a comprehensive view of how MazaoHub integrates into the broader agricultural ecosystem, delivering tangible value to both farmers and agribusinesses. This due diligence trip highlighted our commitment to transparency, sustainable growth, and measurable impact.

Visits to MazaoHub's Farmer Excellence Center (FEC) at Ifakara

During our visit to Ifakara, we hosted representatives from Elea Foundation at MazaoHub's Farmer Excellence Center (FEC), known as "Kliniki za Kilimo." These centers are more than just tech hubs; they are places of trust and collaboration, where farmers receive the personalized support they need. The visit was pivotal in showcasing our work on the ground, and the representatives engaged directly with farmers and our field team to see the impact for themselves.

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

Honoring the Visit from AlphaMundi Foundation

01 Nov, 2024  ·  Admin

On 28th October 2024, MazaoHub had the honor of hosting representatives from AlphaMundi Foundation, an organization committed to driving social impact investments that empower businesses and communities. Their focus on sustainable development and financial inclusion aligns closely with our mission, making this visit a significant milestone in our journey.

Starting the journey at Mtwara Farmer Excellence Center

The visit began at our Mtwara Farmer Excellence Center, strategically located at the market near Masjid Albany Mosque. We chose this location deliberately to be close to where farmers deliver their goods, making us more accessible and visible to them. As farmers visit the market to trade, they can easily approach our center and inquire about the services we offer.

During the visit, AlphaMundi's Nora and Clair were thrilled to learn about our operations and explore our soil testing technology. The opportunity to witness how we automate real-time data collection and analyze critical soil metrics within minutes left them impressed by our commitment to precision agriculture.

Engaging with farmers: Mr. Mohammed's journey

After the center visit, we headed to meet one of the farmers benefiting from MazaoHub's support. Mr. Mohammed, a cashew nut farmer managing 2 acres of land, welcomed us to his farm. While his primary crop is cashew nut trees, he also practices intercropping with cassava to diversify his production. Mr. Mohammed expressed his gratitude for our agronomist, Jason, who has been providing tailored guidance to help him increase his yields. As an experienced agronomist, Jason focuses on a comprehensive approach to cashew nut farming, advising on best practices to maximize productivity and sustainability.

Field Notes  /  Story

A Cry of a Woman — Her Farm, Her Story

22 Nov, 2024  ·  Admin

It is well known that agriculture is the backbone of the nation, and women are the major contributors to it. Yet it's sad to see that she is still in tears — she makes a huge contribution but goes unrecognized due to systemic inequalities that keep her from achieving her full potential. Despite their significant role in producing food and sustaining communities, women face challenges that are both profound and deeply entrenched.

One of the most glaring issues is limited land ownership. Globally, women make up 36% of the workforce in agrifood systems, with figures as high as 66% in sub-Saharan Africa. Yet they own a mere 10–20% of agricultural land. This disparity robs women of the ability to fully participate in decision-making and invest in their livelihoods, keeping them dependent and marginalized in a sector they dominate. How could that be fair?

Financial barriers further entrench these inequalities. Women often lack access to the credit, inputs, and resources they need to scale their agricultural activities. Without financial independence, they remain trapped in cycles of poverty, unable to purchase quality seeds, fertilizers, or equipment. These constraints limit their productivity and deepen their vulnerability in a rapidly changing agricultural landscape.

The impacts of climate change amplify these challenges. Women are disproportionately affected by climate-related risks due to:

  • Higher levels of poverty
  • Lower education rates
  • Limited access to information and services

They often bear the brunt of environmental degradation and extreme weather events, lacking the tools and resources to recover from these shocks. While their role in agriculture makes them indispensable, the system continues to fail them. At MazaoHub, we are committed to changing that — putting data, agronomy, inputs, finance and market access directly into the hands of women farmers, so that her cry becomes her victory.

Share

Field Notes  /  Soil

The Crucial Link Between Soil Health and Human Health

30 Jan, 2025  ·  Admin

Soil health and human health are intricately linked. At MazaoHub, we recognize that sustainable agriculture begins with maintaining and restoring soil vitality. Our precision agronomy services, soil testing, and farmer education initiatives ensure that farmers have the necessary tools to cultivate nutrient-dense crops that enhance human well-being. Below, we explore how we actively integrate soil health management into our core mission to create a healthier society.

1. Enhancing nutrient-dense crops through precision agronomy

Healthy soils are the foundation of nutritious food production. We prioritize advanced soil testing and customized fertilization plans that help our farmers optimize soil nutrients. This ensures that crops absorb essential elements like nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium, which are critical for human nutrition. Our services empower farmers to produce food that is richer in vitamins and minerals, contributing to improved dietary quality in communities.

2. Ensuring micronutrient availability for improved health

Micronutrients such as zinc, iron, and selenium are crucial for human health, and their presence in crops depends on soil health. Through data-driven soil management, we guide farmers on how to replenish and maintain these critical micronutrients in their fields. By applying organic amendments and biofortification techniques, we are actively combating nutritional deficiencies that lead to health conditions such as anemia and weakened immune systems.

3. Improving diet quality through sustainable soil practices

We are also at the forefront of promoting regenerative agriculture practices that restore soil fertility and structure — leading to better, more resilient harvests, more nutritious food, and ultimately healthier diets and healthier communities.

Share

Field Notes  /  Agronomy

Why Africa's Rural Farmers Keep Failing: The Destruction Caused by Guesswork Agronomy

24 Feb, 2025  ·  Admin

At MazaoHub, agronomy service isn't just a department — it's the core of everything we do. Just as McDonald's and KFC have signature food formulas, our Agronomy Service Delivery Model is our signature, setting the gold standard for rural farming in Africa. We don't just provide advice — we become part of the farmer's life, ensuring that every touchpoint, from soil testing and recommendations to crop health management and harvesting, is a seamless and transformative experience. When anyone visits a farm we support, they should immediately recognize the MazaoHub effect: flourishing crops, optimized resources, and inspired farmers.

Why agronomy service is our core business

Farming success is not about luck. It is not just about planting seeds and waiting for nature to do its work. It is about precision, planning, and execution. Soil intelligence is important, but it is only the beginning. Agronomy service delivery ensures that every farmer receives personalized, data-driven, and hands-on support from pre-planting to post-harvest — ensuring maximum yields, profitability, and sustainability. Without proper agronomy service, farmers operate in the dark, guessing their way through trial and error, leading to wasted resources, low productivity, environmental damage and significant financial losses.

MazaoHub transforms farming into a well-oiled system that delivers consistent, predictable, and scalable success. It is not just about increasing yields — it is about creating an ecosystem where farmers thrive, agribusinesses prosper, and food security becomes a reality.

The 6P Agronomy Service Delivery Model

Our agronomy service delivery model follows the 6P framework, ensuring that every farm and every farmer receives consistent, structured, end-to-end agronomy support — from understanding the land, to prescribing the right plan, to delivering inputs, protecting the crop, and ensuring the farmer profits.

Field Notes  /  Soil

It Begins with the Soil… but It Ends with Hope

29 Apr, 2025  ·  Admin

Yes, it's harvest time. And now, it's market time. But what you see here — onions freshly harvested — is more than just a crop. It's a story. A story of a farmer who believed. A story of an agronomist who stood by them, day by day, soil test after soil test, through every rain and every ray of sun.

Together, with MazaoHub, they walked the journey. And now, as we complete our Crop Trading pilot and unveil our Agiza Mazao — Crop Sourcing Centers — we are not just connecting crops to markets; we are connecting lives to purpose.

This is the MazaoHub journey:

  • Soil first — We begin where most don't: testing, mapping, and understanding the land.
  • Personalized agronomy — AI-powered insights, local wisdom, and real agronomists guiding every move.
  • Digital planning & farm tools — Precision farming in the palm of your hand, even offline.
  • Access to inputs & machines — From seeds to fertilizers to tractor leasing, we deliver solutions, not just products.
  • Field monitoring & extension support — Consistent follow-ups, data-driven visits, and a call away for any question.
  • Harvest support — Post-harvest care, loss prevention, and direct linkage to markets through our Crop Sourcing Centers.

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

MazaoHub Hosted a Powerful Gathering in Mlali Ward

05 May, 2025  ·  Admin

The MazaoHub team hosted a powerful gathering in Mlali ward, bringing together 200+ farmers from eight villages, representatives from MVIWATA (Mtandao wa Vikundi vya Wakulima Tanzania), and key agricultural stakeholders in Mvomero District, Morogoro Region.

With warm welcoming remarks from local leaders and an inspiring session led by our CEO, Mr. Geophrey Tenganamba, the event highlighted the transformative potential of MazaoHub's Farmer Excellence Centers — or, as we call them, Kliniki za Kilimo — in delivering agronomy services, market linkages, and future agri-finance access.

We see ourselves not just as a service provider but also as a bridge for smallholder farmers striving to rise above the challenges of poor agronomy services, unreliable input supplies, weak infrastructure, and limited access to market information. Through our continued engagement at the village level, we are building lasting relationships of trust — and turning gatherings like this one into real, measurable change on the farm.

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

Why Hollywood-Scale Collaboration Is the Key to Empowering Rural Farmers

06 May, 2025  ·  Admin

In a world where climate change is accelerating at an alarming rate, the livelihoods of rural farmers are under unprecedented threat. These farmers, who form the backbone of global food security, face challenges ranging from unpredictable weather patterns, soil degradation, pests, and a lack of close professional support, to rising costs of agricultural inputs. At the same time, research institutions, agritechs, NGOs, food companies, governments, and scientists are working tirelessly to generate vast amounts of valuable data and knowledge aimed at mitigating these challenges. The critical question is: how do we bring all these players together to ensure that data and insights reach the farmers who need it most, in the most practical and transformative ways?

To answer this, let's take inspiration from an unexpected source — Hollywood. Imagine watching a blockbuster movie. As the credits roll, you see hundreds of names and dozens of organizations: artists, companies, NGOs, government agencies, even local authorities. Hundreds of people came together to create just 90 minutes of cinematic magic. The amount of coordination and collaboration is staggering.

Now, think about the agriculture sector. This is our movie. It's a much larger production — one that directly impacts billions of lives. But, just like a movie, it requires collaboration at every level. And the key to that collaboration is ensuring that research and data are not left on the shelf, but delivered directly to the people who need them most: farmers.

Across the globe, countless institutions conduct cutting-edge research on crops, soil science, plant diseases, water management, and post-harvest practices. Institutions like CGIAR Climate Action — which brings together centers such as CIMMYT, the International Rice Research Institute, and the International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA) — are making significant advancements in agricultural science. The challenge has always been the "last mile": connecting that science to the smallholder farmer. That is exactly the role MazaoHub plays — the production house that brings every collaborator together and delivers the science to the field.

Share

Field Notes  /  Story

I Grew Up on This Family Land

06 May, 2025  ·  Admin

When I was young, my father used to farm on this land. I grew up here, on this family land — learning the rhythm of the seasons, the smell of the soil after the first rains, and the weight of a harvest carried home.

This land fed us. It paid for school. It held our hopes. But it also held our struggles. Year after year, we farmed the way our parents farmed — by memory, by guesswork, by hope — without soil testing, without timely advice, without a fair market waiting at the end of the season. When the rains failed or the middlemen cheated, the whole family felt it.

Standing on this same land today, I see both the pain and the promise. The promise is that everything that was missing then is possible now: affordable soil testing, AI-powered agronomy in a local app, quality inputs nearby, and buyers who pay fairly and on time.

This is why MazaoHub exists — so that the next family growing up on land like this will not inherit the same struggles, but a system built for them. The land that raised me is the reason I do this work.

Share

← Back to all Field Notes

Field Notes  /  Story

Tears of the Rukwa Fields

07 May, 2025  ·  Admin

The MazaoHub journey across Tanzania unveils realities beyond expectations. This week, we landed in the Kalambo district, Rukwa region, home to seven villages: Kanyezi, Kasanga, Katazi, Katete, Kilesha, Kisumba, and Legezamwendo. The agricultural potential here is nothing short of extraordinary. Almost any crop imaginable can thrive in this land blessed with abundant water. Yet, a heart-wrenching truth unfolds as we delve into the lives of the farmers.

Trial and error, not triumph

These villages are trapped in a cycle of "trial and error" agriculture. Maize and cassava, the dominant crops, are grown not for profit, but for survival. Erratic markets leave farmers with little to no control over their income. Maize, their staple food, is often sold at nearby milling centers for a pittance, subject to unpredictable price swings. This constant uncertainty cripples their ability to plan, invest, and escape the clutches of subsistence farming.

Charcoal: a desperate gamble

Driven by the desperation for income, some farmers have turned to a devastating practice: charcoal production. Instead of reaping the rewards of cultivation, they are forced to chop down trees and burn them into charcoal, scarring the very land that sustains them. This environmental tragedy is a stark testament to the lack of alternative income sources and the absence of guidance towards profitable, sustainable farming.

A tapestry of untapped abundance

The potential of these villages is staggering. Over 20 different types of crops can flourish here, including maize, rice, beans, wheat, oilseeds, sunflower, potatoes, bananas, soybeans, cashews, coffee and tea. With the right agronomy, data and market linkages, the tears of the Rukwa fields can become a story of triumph — and that is the future MazaoHub is working to build, village by village.

Share

Field Notes  /  Field Force

The Uber for Agronomists Is Here

09 May, 2025  ·  Admin

Welcome to MazaoHub — your Uber for extension services

At MazaoHub, we're transforming agriculture through care, connection, and cutting-edge technology. As a data-driven agronomy service provider, we empower farmers using a simple yet powerful three-step approach.

1. Understand the soil and climate

We produce affordable, portable soil testing kits and integrate remote sensing tools to collect accurate climate and farm data — because every successful harvest starts with understanding the land.

2. Empower farmers with smart farming tools

Our MazaoHub App works both online and offline, giving farmers full control over their season with features like:

  • Farm Planning — step-by-step guidance from land preparation to harvest.
  • Knowledge Hub — practical, localized farming tips and best practices.
  • Farming Manager — record farm activities and track expenses.
  • Order Inputs — source certified inputs from nearby Farmer Excellence Centers.
  • Farm Analytics — AI-powered insights for better decision-making.
  • Farmer Forum — connect with fellow farmers to learn and grow together.

3. Bring support closer with Farmer Excellence Centers

These are physical agricultural clinics — either run by MazaoHub agronomy staff or franchised through local agro-dealers, co-ops, or partners trained in our service delivery model — so that expert help is always just around the corner from the farm.

Share

Field Notes  /  Agronomy

MazaoHub Agronomy Software: Smart, Local Tools for Real Farmers

10 May, 2025  ·  Admin

MazaoHub Agronomy Software offers practical tools for real farming — a system that works in the village, offline or online. Designed for farmers, agronomists, field officers, and Farmer Excellence Centers (FECs), every feature speaks the language of the land.

1. Farm Planning — everything starts here

Once a farm is registered and mapped, the system provides a full plan based on location, crop, and season. Recommended seeds, fertilizers, herbicides, and tools appear automatically. An instant cost estimate, profit forecast, and buyer suggestions are included. Agronomists assist with validation, even for those without smartphones.

2. Knowledge Hub — localized agronomy guidance

Tailored farming advice based on crop, soil, village, and season. Different villages receive different guidance based on real conditions. It covers soil health, pests, climate adaptation, and regenerative practices — simplifying research into practical tips farmers can apply instantly.

3. Place an order — inputs based on real needs

After planning, the system connects to partner agro-dealers with the right inputs. Seeds, fertilizers, and tools are ordered directly based on the farm's prescription — so farmers buy exactly what their land and crop require, no more guesswork and no waste.

Share

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

Why We Started MazaoHub — and Why It Matters Now More Than Ever

13 May, 2025  ·  Admin

I was raised by a smallholder farmer. My grandmother was my first teacher in agriculture — not in a classroom, but in the fields, under the sun, bending over tomato plants, wiping sweat with the back of her hand, and praying the rains would come on time.

She never used words like "data" or "climate resilience." But I watched her suffer because she didn't have access to the things we now know are possible: timely advice, affordable soil testing, access to quality inputs, or a market that treats her fairly. She worked so hard — like millions of farmers across Africa — and still, harvest after harvest, she remained stuck in poverty. That reality broke something inside me. And it planted a seed.

That seed became MazaoHub. MazaoHub was born out of pain — and out of love. Love for our farmers, our soil, our future. And pain from watching potential go to waste because the system wasn't built for them. What we're building today isn't just a company. It's a quiet revolution — starting in the villages, carried by agronomists, powered by data, rooted in dignity.

What is MazaoHub really about?

MazaoHub is where technology meets touch — where satellites and sensors meet real agronomists and real farmers. It is precision agriculture with a human heart: diagnosing the soil, prescribing the right plan, delivering the right inputs, and standing beside the farmer from planting to market. That is why it matters now, more than ever.

Share

Field Notes  /  Traceability

A Story of Trust and Traceable Harvests

15 May, 2025  ·  Admin

There was a time — not long ago — when food companies, schools, processors, millers, hotels, and exporters across Africa were desperately trying to source crops from rural farmers. Some tried their luck by sending money to brokers in villages. Others used informal middlemen with no system, no record, and no guarantee. And the stories were always the same:

"We sent money to a contact in the village, but they vanished."
"They don't pick up their phones anymore."
"We waited weeks, but no crops ever came."
"We ordered 10,000 metric tons of maize. What did we get? 300 tons. And half of it was poor quality. Is this a joke? Maybe we should start farming ourselves."

This story repeats every season. Substandard produce. Lost money. Ghost middlemen. Empty warehouses. It's not just a supply chain issue — it's a broken trust issue. In the end, buyers lost millions, trust was broken, and worse — farmers who worked so hard were never paid fairly.

This is the broken system that MazaoHub's Crop Sourcing Centers — Agiza Mazao — were built to fix. Every harvest is traceable from the farm that grew it. Every farmer is paid fairly and on time. Every buyer is guaranteed the quality and quantity they ordered. Trust, finally, becomes part of the supply chain.

Share

Field Notes  /  Inputs

Agrovets Powered by MazaoHub: From Shops to Smart Farming Hubs

27 May, 2025  ·  Admin

In today's farming landscape, agrovets are more than just sellers of seeds and chemicals — they're trusted community advisors, connectors, and lifelines for farmers in both remote and urban areas.

But let's be honest: even the most passionate agrovet faces daily challenges — stockouts, low sales, poor farmer trust, product expiry, and little support from suppliers or extension services.

That's exactly why MazaoHub built the Pembejeo Connect & Agrovet System — a powerful platform that transforms ordinary input shops into smart, climate-resilient, and farmer-centered hubs known as Kliniki za Kilimo (Farmer Excellence Centers). This is more than software. This is a movement. A shift. A revolution of hope.

What is Pembejeo Connect & Agrovet System?

It's an agronomy service platform. It's a digital retail management tool. It's a supply network. It's a bridge between agrovets and thousands of farmers — and it's designed for you, whether you have capital or just a dream.

Here's what it offers:

  • Digital shop management — even from your phone, even from your home.
  • Soil testing tools and professional fertilizer advice — turning every sale into a science-backed recommendation.
  • Real-time stock sharing between partner agrovets — so a farmer never leaves empty-handed; if you don't have it, a nearby partner does.
  • Direct access to a network of farmers already on the MazaoHub platform, driving demand to your shop.
Share

Field Notes  /  Soil

Batch 3 Ready: 390 MazaoHub Soil Kits for Smarter, Profitable Farming

23 Jun, 2025  ·  Admin

Batch 3 of Soil Kits is ready — 390 units of precision power! We are proud to announce the completion of our 3rd batch of high-quality MazaoHub Soil Kits, built to transform farming across the region. These kits are not just tools — they're a revolution in the hands of independent extension officers and farmers.

We're welcoming independent extension officers to join the MazaoHub network. We offer:

  • A ready network of farmers
  • Revenue-sharing for soil testing services
  • A powerful agronomy platform with AI-driven farm business plans, crop insights, automatic records, and real-time plant health tracking

All data flows directly into CropSupply.com, linking farmers to transparent markets.

Everyone wins

  • Farmers get better yields and real markets.
  • Extension officers earn predictable income.
  • Agrovets sell more inputs, backed by science and a trusted network.

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

MazaoHub's Big Moment at Nane Nane 2025

11 Aug, 2025  ·  Admin

If you've been with us for a while, you know that every Nane Nane Agricultural Exhibition tells a part of the MazaoHub story. But this year — this year was something special.

Let's take a quick journey back. In 2022, we were strangers to many. Our CEO was the only one who made it to the exhibition, carrying nothing but a laptop, a dream, and a story about saving smallholder farmers. Few people stopped at our small stand. Fewer still believed that a farm clinic — a "hospital for farms" — could change rural agriculture.

Fast forward to Nane Nane 2025 in Dodoma. This year we launched MazaoHub 2.0.2 — a more powerful, more local, more connected version of our platform — in front of farmers, partners, government leaders and the press. From one person at a stand in 2022, we arrived as a team, backed by a network of Farmer Excellence Centers, soil kits in the field, thousands of farmers onboarded, and real partnerships with banks and food companies.

From humble beginnings to a new era for farmers — Nane Nane 2025 was proof that the quiet revolution we started in the villages is now being seen, trusted, and scaled. And we are only getting started.

Share

Field Notes  /  Partnership

A Historic Partnership: MazaoHub × CRDB Foundation

11 Sep, 2025  ·  Admin  ·  3 min read

Today, history is written. MazaoHub has officially partnered with CRDB Bank Foundation, opening a new chapter where farmers will no longer be locked out of opportunity simply because they lack traditional collateral.

For years, thousands of farmers — already within MazaoHub's network — could not access our services because they had no capital. Now, together with CRDB Foundation, we are unlocking the door for all.

Through this partnership, farmers will no longer be judged by collateral under the traditional banking system. Smallholder farmers will gain access to finance powered by MazaoHub's agronomy data — from the soil beneath their feet, to the health of their crops, to the inputs they need — validated in real time through our Farmer Excellence Centers (Kliniki za Kilimo) across 15 regions. With this, they can finally access precision farming tools, agronomy services, inputs and markets to transform their yields and livelihoods.

We are deeply grateful to Ms. Tully Esther Mwambapa, Managing Director of CRDB Foundation, and to Mr. Abdulmajid M. Nsekela, Managing Director of CRDB Bank Group, for their visionary leadership in making this possible.

We extend our heartfelt thanks to the Government of Tanzania, led by Madam President Samia Suluhu Hassan, for recognizing MazaoHub as a proud beneficiary of the Building a Better Tomorrow (BBT) initiative, and to the Ministry of Agriculture, led by Hon. Hussein Bashe, for championing innovation that touches the lives of farmers at scale.

This MoU is not the end — it is the beginning. We invite more partners, institutions and friends to walk this journey with us. Together, we can build stronger pathways of capital and data-driven agronomy services to transform millions of lives.

From soil to market.   From data to opportunity.   From partnership to transformation.

Share

Field Notes  /  Funding

MazaoHub Closes $2M Oversubscribed Seed Round

23 Sep, 2025  ·  Admin  ·  4 min read

We are happy to announce that MazaoHub has closed a $2 million oversubscribed Seed round — comprising $1.5M in equity led by Catalyst Fund with participation from Nordic Impact Fund, Mercy Corps Ventures, elea Foundation, Impacc and DOB Equity, and $500k in non-dilutive capital from the Livelihood Impact Fund — a strong signal of growing investor appetite for blended finance in climate adaptation.

MazaoHub operates a hybrid Tech & Touch model that fuses soil sensors, portable soil kits and AI-powered farm-management software with on-the-ground agronomists and direct market access. Our offline-first platform equips farmers with crop dashboards, cost-analysis tools and daily checklists built for low-connectivity environments, while rural agribusinesses are upgraded into Farmer Excellence Centers — "agricultural clinics" where professional agronomists deliver in-person guidance informed by digital insights. At harvest, CropSupply.com connects farmers directly to buyers and ensures full traceability from soil data to shipment.

For investors, this round validates a system that turns data into execution: diagnostics from sensors feed the software; agronomists translate insights into field practices; Farmer Excellence Centers institutionalize quality and record-keeping; and CropSupply.com links verified production to demand.

Our model is intentionally built to deliver both productivity gains and climate benefits. By reducing fertilizer use by up to 30%, boosting organic-manure adoption fivefold and optimizing irrigation, farmers grow more food with fewer inputs while lowering emissions, saving water and cutting energy use.

As Geophrey Tenganamba, CEO and Co-Founder of MazaoHub, explains: "Farmers become data-driven decision-makers, buyers gain trusted traceability, and agribusinesses operate as climate-smart franchises. This is where sustainability meets scale."

"MazaoHub is showing that African agriculture can blend data insights with local agronomists to enable sustainable farming at scale," said Maelis Carraro, Founder and Managing Partner at Catalyst Fund.

Share

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

Jiunge na Mfumo wa Tech & Touch wa MazaoHub

25 Sep, 2025  ·  Admin

Mimi ni wewe. Wewe ni mimi.

Kama wewe ni kijana, afisa ugani au mjasiriamali mwenye ujuzi wa kilimo, huu ni wakati wako. Sisi MazaoHub tunatoa nafasi ya kipekee kupitia mfumo wetu wa Tech & Touch, ili kukujengea msingi endelevu wa kuhudumia wakulima kwa kutumia data halisi, masoko halisi, na kipato halisi.

Huko mkoani Manyara, tayari wakulima wamechagua Tech & Touch kwa sababu wanaona thamani yake — kuanzia maandalizi ya shamba hadi mavuno, pamoja na wanunuzi wa mazao wakiwa tayari baada ya mavuno. Wakulima wanalipia kwa sababu wanaona matokeo.

Na sasa tunaeneza fursa hii kote nchini. Tunakaribisha vijana, maafisa ugani na wajasiriamali wa kilimo kujiunga na mtandao wa MazaoHub — kupata vifaa vya kupima udongo, mfumo wa ushauri wa kilimo unaotumia AI, mafunzo, na mgawanyo wa mapato kutoka kwa huduma za upimaji udongo na ushauri kwa wakulima.

Ukiwa na Kliniki ya Kilimo (Farmer Excellence Center) karibu na wakulima, unakuwa daraja kati ya sayansi na shamba — na unajenga kipato chako huku ukibadilisha maisha ya wakulima. Huu ni wakati wako.

Share

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

Turning a TSh 1.2M Loss into Profit

20 Oct, 2025  ·  Admin

Yesterday in Tukuyu, Mbeya, we stood with a farmer whose entire livelihood is the soil.

Last season, on 4 acres of avocado, he spent TSh 3,200,000 and earned only TSh 2,000,000 — a TSh 1,200,000 loss (−37.5% margin). Productivity, productivity, productivity. He wasn't under MazaoHub then.

In December last year, he joined MazaoHub for his 8-acre banana blocks. We mapped his fields, ran soil tests, and built season-by-season farm plans — calibrating N–K nutrition for bunch fill, setting de-suckering and sanitation routines, irrigation scheduling, and harvest windows.

We converted his soil and farm data into a credit profile and, with CRDB Foundation, piloted farmer loans right here. He told us bananas have already covered last year's avocado losses — and he thanked us with a bunch of his own bananas.

This is what the "hospital for farms" model does: it turns guesswork into a plan, a plan into a harvest, and a harvest into a credit history — turning a loss into a profit, one farmer at a time.

Share

Field Notes  /  Vision

Are We Crazy Enough to Build the Alibaba of African Agriculture?

30 Dec, 2025  ·  Admin  ·  5 min read

We did not go to Zanzibar for a holiday. We went there to ask ourselves a hard question: Are we crazy?

Because when you say you want to build "the Alibaba of agriculture" from dusty rural villages, with farmers who don't always have smartphones or even basic trust in systems… it does sound like madness. And yet, that is exactly what MazaoHub is trying to do.

A team building that was not about games

Last week, more than 45 of us — agronomists on motorbikes, call-center teams, data people, outreach officers, HQ staff — came together for a retreat. Yes, there was the ocean; yes, there was laughter. But the real agenda was heavier: to remember our vision and decide, again, if we are truly ready to build it. We did not speak as "employees". We spoke as builders.

We sat with a big, uncomfortable idea: what if MazaoHub is not just a startup, but a system that must still be alive 25, 50, even 100 years from now?

The dream: an integrated system for African farmers

We want to create a fully integrated, data-driven system for African agriculture — where a farmer in a remote village is matched to a real extension officer, not just an app. Together they use a digital agronomy system covering soil intelligence, crop and variety selection, spacing, fertilization and IPM, record-keeping and harvest planning.

Every action becomes data — soil status, inputs used, planting dates, pests observed, yield per acre. That data guides what inputs are needed, where and when; powers a distribution system where only verified agrovets and partner Farmer Excellence Centers supply those inputs; helps wholesale distributors send seeds and fertilizers based on real demand; and flows straight into CropSupply.com, where buyers — local and global — see harvest forecasts, quality and traceability reports, and buy crops seamlessly from farmers.

One integrated system. One flow of data. One story of the farmer. That is the dream — and the honest question is whether we can actually build it, or are just dreaming loudly.

Share

Field Notes  /  Field Notes

A Statement That We Believe in Union Growth

16 Feb, 2026  ·  Admin

On the 12th of February, the MazaoHub team made a statement: a statement that we believe in union growth. A statement that when people in the agriculture (crop farming) value chain work together with standards and integrity, we win together.

This week in Morogoro, at MazaoHouse, we hosted a strategic session between MazaoHub, our CropSupply Aggregators, and representatives from Capwell Industries — one of East Africa's most respected food companies — followed by a lunch session at Morena Hotel.

For those not so familiar with Capwell Industries in Kenya, these are a food manufacturing company that has set the bar high in food, health, and nutrition. They are serious about standards. Serious about systems. Serious about quality. And that is exactly why we partnered.

Around the table were 14 suppliers capable of supplying up to 50,000 MT of maize and other produce, our MazaoHub team, and Capwell's representatives. We discussed a clear framework for June–October 2026 maize supply, because serious markets require serious systems.

We openly discussed challenges, standards, pricing and quality requirements — and aligned on a transparent, traceable model where farmers, aggregators and buyers all grow together. This is what union growth looks like: not competition that crushes, but collaboration that lifts the whole value chain.

Share

Field Notes  /  Inputs

What the Argus Fertilizer Africa Conference 2026 Means for a Data-Driven Fertilizer Market

16 Feb, 2026  ·  Admin

Today we concluded the Argus Fertilizer Africa Conference, a high-impact gathering that brings together the full fertilizer and agricultural input value chain across Africa — fertilizer producers, traders, importers, blenders, distributors, logistics companies, agribusinesses, financiers, and policymakers — with a shared goal: make fertilizer supply, affordability, access, and usage more effective for Africa's farmers.

The conference is practical and business-focused, built around three core outcomes:

  1. Networking and deal-making across the supply chain — commercial partnerships, distribution agreements, logistics arrangements, and financing relationships.
  2. Market, price, and trade intelligence sharing — how fertilizer markets are moving, where shortages and oversupplies exist, what is changing in regional trade, and what this means for planning and investment.
  3. Strategies to scale fertilizer supply and adoption — not just at policy level, but through solutions that actually work at the "last mile," where most African farmers operate.

For me, this was my first time attending. But it immediately became clear that this is not just another conference — it is the kind of platform where serious stakeholders meet, align, and move capital, inventory, and distribution decisions.

Why MazaoHub's "Tech & Touch" model fits the moment

The whole industry is moving toward a data-driven fertilizer market — pricing, demand and distribution guided by real information rather than guesswork. But data at the top of the value chain only matters if it reaches the bottom. That is exactly where MazaoHub fits: our soil sensors and AI agronomy generate real, farm-level demand data, while our Farmer Excellence Centers (the "Touch") deliver the right product, in the right dose, to the right farm, at the right time. In a data-driven fertilizer market, MazaoHub is the bridge between the warehouse and the soil.

Share
